blocks.subtitle: Transport, drainage, and defense throughout the body

blocks.text: The **Circulatory System** transports blood containing **oxygen** and **nutrients** throughout the body and picks up **metabolic waste products** for disposal.

blocks.definition: Carries blood between the **heart** and the **lungs** for **gas exchange**.
Carries blood between the **heart** and the rest of the **body's tissues**.

blocks.text: The **heart** is the major structure of the circulatory system. It is a **muscular organ** that pumps blood through **contraction** and **circulates** it throughout the body. It is located in the **thoracic cavity** behind and slightly to the **left of the sternum**, between the lungs.

blocks.definition: **Supports and lubricates** the heart during contraction.
The **upper chambers** on each side of the heart.

items.text: **Right Atrioventricular (AV) valve**, also called the **Tricuspid valve**
**Left Atrioventricular (AV) valve**, also called the **Bicuspid valve** or **Mitral valve**

blocks.text: **Arteries** are blood vessels that have **three discrete layers** surrounding the **lumen**, or the space in which blood flows: the **tunica adventitia**, or outer layer; the **tunica media**, or middle layer; and the **tunica intima**, or inner layer.

**Arteries** are blood vessels that carry blood **away from the heart**. They have **thick walls** because the blood that moves through them is under **pressure** from the contraction of the ventricles.
Arteries branch into smaller vessels called **arterioles**, which ultimately branch to form **capillaries**.

blocks.definition: **Lack of oxygen** in the arteries that causes **damage to the muscle**.
Occurs when the heart receives **inadequate blood supply** through the **coronary arteries**.

blocks.text: **Capillaries** are composed only of the **tunica intima**, a **single layer of endothelial cells**. This allows **rapid diffusion** of gases and nutrients between tissues and blood across the **capillary membrane**.

**Veins** carry blood **back toward the heart**. Veins have **thinner walls** and **less muscle** than arteries do, because they do not experience large fluctuations in blood pressure. Veins are **closer to the surface** than are arteries.

rows: **Thick walls**; three layers under **pressure** from ventricular contraction
Only the **tunica intima** — a **single layer of endothelial cells**
**Thinner walls**, **less muscle**; located **closer to the surface**
blocks.caption: Comparison of arteries, capillaries, and veins

blocks.text: Circulatory Anatomy of the Antecubital Fossa

The **Antecubital Fossa** is the area just **distal to the elbow joint** where blood is usually drawn. Becoming familiar with the anatomy of this area will help you **draw blood safely and confidently**.

blocks.title: Key Points
items: The **Circulatory System** transports oxygen and nutrients and removes metabolic waste.
**Pulmonary circulation** serves the lungs; **systemic circulation** serves the rest of the body.
Chambers: **Atria** (upper) and **Ventricles** (lower), divided by the **interatrial** and **interventricular septa**.
**Arteries** carry blood away from the heart (thick walls); **veins** return blood (thinner walls); **capillaries** enable diffusion.
The **Antecubital Fossa**, distal to the elbow, is the usual site for **blood draws**.
